title: Remove Set Items
content:
### Removing Items from a Set

---

### `.remove()` -- Remove by Value

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
fruits.remove("banana")
print(fruits)  # {"apple", "cherry"}
```

> **Error:** Raises `KeyError` if item does not exist.

---

### `.discard()` -- Remove by Value (Safe)

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
fruits.discard("banana")   # Removes it
fruits.discard("grape")    # No error! (silent no-op)
print(fruits)  # {"apple", "cherry"}
```

---

### `.pop()` -- Remove Random Item

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
removed = fruits.pop()  # Removes and returns a random item
print(removed)
print(fruits)
```

> Sets are unordered, so `.pop()` removes an **arbitrary** item.

---

### `.clear()` -- Empty the Set

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
fruits.clear()
print(fruits)  # set()
```

---

### Difference `-` Operator

```python
set1 = {1, 2, 3, 4}
set2 = {3, 4, 5}
result = set1 - set2  # Elements in set1 but not in set2
print(result)  # {1, 2}
```

> **Best Practice:** Use `.discard()` when you are not sure if the item exists. Use `.remove()` when you expect it to be there.
codeExample:
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}

fruits.discard("banana")
fruits.discard("grape")  # No error
print("After discards:", fruits)

fruits.add("kiwi")
removed = fruits.pop()
print("Popped:", removed)
print("Remaining:", fruits)

# Difference
set1 = {1, 2, 3, 4}
set2 = {3, 4, 5}
print("Difference:", set1 - set2)

quiz.question: Which method removes an item but does NOT raise an error if it doesn't exist?
